questions.questionText: Which station should serve as the control when evaluating the effect of fertilizer runoff on dissolved oxygen?

answerOptions.rationale: It is unaffected by farms or town runoff, so it isolates natural conditions.
This is the impacted site, not the control.
This location adds new variables (treated wastewater).
Controls must be a single baseline condition.

questions.passage: Students investigate whether increased fertilizer runoff is lowering dissolved oxygen (DO) in a river. They set three sample stations: upstream forest, midstream near farms, and downstream near a town outflow. Each day they record water temperature, nitrates (mg/L), and DO (mg/L). They also note that algae blooms appear most frequently near the farms.

questions.questionText: If nitrates spike midstream while temperature stays constant, which claim best explains a drop in dissolved oxygen?

answerOptions.text: Cooler water upstream dissolves less oxygen.
answerOptions.rationale: Cooler water dissolves more oxygen, not less.

answerOptions.text: Excess nutrients fuel algae that consume oxygen when they decompose.
answerOptions.rationale: Decomposition of algal blooms lowers DO.

answerOptions.text: Higher river velocity midstream forces oxygen out of solution.
answerOptions.rationale: Faster water usually increases oxygen mixing.
Town runoff is downstream, not midstream.

questions.questionText: Based on the data, which light color appears to be least effective for bean plant growth?

answerOptions.rationale: Red light showed higher or comparable growth metrics (15 cm height increase, 10 new leaves, 5 leaf color intensity) to the control and other colors, indicating it is effective, not least effective.
Blue light showed good growth metrics (14 cm height increase, 9 new leaves), comparable to the control, indicating it is effective, not least effective.
Green light resulted in the lowest plant height increase (5 cm), fewest new leaves (3), and lowest leaf color intensity (2), making it the least effective for growth among the tested colors.
Yellow light showed moderate growth (10 cm height increase, 7 new leaves), which is not the lowest among the options.

Effect of Different Light Colors on Bean Plant Growth Over Two Weeks

Light ColorPlant Height Increase (cm)Number of New LeavesLeaf Color Intensity (Scale 1-5)
White (Control)1284
Red15105
Blue1494
Green532
Yellow1073

questions.questionText: A student hypothesizes that plants grow best under light colors that are absorbed, rather than reflected. Which statement is best supported by the experimental results?

answerOptions.text: The hypothesis is contradicted because plants grew poorly under green light, which they reflect.
answerOptions.rationale: The hypothesis suggests plants grow best under absorbed light. Plants reflect green light, and the data shows poor growth under green light. This observation supports the hypothesis, as poor growth under reflected light implies good growth under absorbed light. Therefore, the hypothesis is supported, not contradicted.

answerOptions.text: The hypothesis is supported because plants grew well under red and blue light, which are absorbed.
answerOptions.rationale: Plants appear green because they reflect green light, meaning they primarily absorb red and blue light. The data shows that bean plants grew significantly better under red and blue light compared to green light. This directly supports the hypothesis that plants grow best under colors they absorb.

answerOptions.text: The hypothesis is neither supported nor contradicted, as the experiment did not measure light absorption.
answerOptions.rationale: While light absorption wasn't directly measured, the common knowledge that plants reflect green light and absorb other wavelengths, combined with the growth data, allows for an inference that supports the hypothesis. The question asks what is best supported by the results in context of the hypothesis.

answerOptions.text: The hypothesis is contradicted because white light (a mix of all colors) produced the best growth.
answerOptions.rationale: White light serves as a control and contains all colors, including the absorbed red and blue. While it produced good growth, red light actually showed slightly better height increase (15 cm vs 12 cm for white), and green light, which is reflected, showed the worst growth. This does not contradict the hypothesis, but rather aligns with it, as white light includes the beneficial absorbed wavelengths.

questions.questionText: Based on the data, what trend can be observed regarding exoplanets that are NOT in the Habitable Zone?

answerOptions.text: They generally have very short orbital periods.
answerOptions.rationale: Both HD 209458 b (3.5 days) and WASP-12b (1.1 days) are not in the Habitable Zone and exhibit significantly shorter orbital periods compared to those in the Habitable Zone.

answerOptions.text: They are always discovered by the Radial Velocity method.
answerOptions.rationale: HD 209458 b and WASP-12b, both not in the Habitable Zone, were discovered by the Transit method, disproving this statement.

answerOptions.text: Their mass is always less than Earth's.
answerOptions.rationale: HD 209458 b (220 relative to Earth) and WASP-12b (440 relative to Earth) are much more massive than Earth, contradicting this statement.

answerOptions.text: They tend to have very long orbital periods.
answerOptions.rationale: The opposite is observed; exoplanets not in the Habitable Zone in this table have very short orbital periods.

questions.passage: **Observed Properties of Selected Exoplanets**| Exoplanet Name | Discovery Method | Mass (relative to Earth) | Orbital Period (Earth days) | Habitable Zone Status ||Kepler-186f | Transit | 1.1 | 130 | Yes ||HD 209458 b | Transit | 220 | 3.5 | No ||Proxima Centauri b | Radial Velocity | 1.3 | 11 | Yes ||WASP-12b | Transit | 440 | 1.1 | No

questions.questionText: Based on the provided data, which exoplanet is most likely to experience the most extreme surface temperatures?

answerOptions.rationale: Kepler-186f is in the Habitable Zone, suggesting moderate temperatures.
While not in the Habitable Zone, WASP-12b has an even shorter orbital period, indicating it is likely closer to its star and thus hotter.
Proxima Centauri b is in the Habitable Zone, suggesting moderate temperatures.
WASP-12b has the shortest orbital period (1.1 Earth days) and is not in the Habitable Zone, indicating it is extremely close to its star and would experience exceptionally high, extreme temperatures.

answerOptions.text: All drops experience the same air resistance.

questions.questionText: If Drop A and Drop C were released from the same height, which statement would accurately describe their fall?

answerOptions.text: Both would hit the ground at the same time because gravity affects them equally.

answerOptions.text: Drop A would hit the ground first because it has less surface area and greater density relative to its mass.

answerOptions.text: Drop C would hit the ground first because it is lighter.

answerOptions.text: It is impossible to predict without knowing the exact mass of each object.

questions.questionText: What coefficient is needed for O₂ in this equation: CH₄ + __O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O?

answerOptions.rationale: To balance the equation, there must be 4 oxygen atoms on the left side (2 from CO₂ and 2 from 2H₂O). Therefore, the coefficient for O₂ must be 2 (2 x 2 = 4).

questions.questionText: Balance this equation: N₂ + __H₂ → 2NH₃. What coefficient is needed for H₂?

answerOptions.rationale: To balance the equation, there are 2 nitrogen atoms on both sides. On the right, there are 6 hydrogen atoms (2 x 3). Therefore, the coefficient for H₂ must be 3 (3 x 2 = 6).
To balance the equation, there are 2 nitrogen atoms on both sides. On the right, there are 6 hydrogen atoms (2 x 3). Therefore, the coefficient for H₂ must be 3 (3 x 2 = 6).

questions.questionText: Hurricanes are known to form and intensify over warm ocean waters. Considering this information, what is a likely long-term effect of consistently warmer ocean temperatures on marine life and ecosystems?

answerOptions.text: Marine life would experience an overall increase in population size due to faster growth rates.
answerOptions.rationale: While some species might initially benefit, consistently warmer temperatures can stress marine ecosystems, leading to habitat loss, decreased oxygen levels, and reduced biodiversity in the long term for many species.

answerOptions.text: The primary food sources for marine life, such as plankton, would significantly decrease, causing widespread food shortages.
answerOptions.rationale: The relationship between temperature and plankton is complex; warmer waters can lead to stratification, limiting nutrient upwelling and impacting overall productivity, but 'significantly decrease' is not a universal rule for all plankton types.

answerOptions.text: Coral reefs may experience widespread bleaching, and the geographical distribution of some marine species could shift.
answerOptions.rationale: Warmer ocean temperatures are a major cause of coral bleaching, where corals lose their symbiotic algae and can die. This also forces temperature-sensitive marine species to move to cooler waters, altering their habitats and ecosystem balances.

answerOptions.text: The acidity of ocean water would decrease, providing a more stable environment for shell-forming organisms.
answerOptions.rationale: Warmer oceans generally lead to less oxygen solubility and are linked to ocean acidification, which increases acidity (decreases pH), making it harder for shell-forming organisms.

stimulusImage.alt: Satellite image of a hurricane from space with a table listing average temperatures of large bodies of water.

questions.questionText: In a forest ecosystem, sunlight allows oak trees to produce acorns. Squirrels eat the acorns. Hawks, in turn, hunt and eat the squirrels. When a hawk dies, its body is broken down by bacteria and fungi, which return nutrients to the soil. In this food web, what would be the most likely immediate effect if a disease wiped out most of the squirrel population?

answerOptions.text: The oak tree population would decrease.
answerOptions.rationale: With fewer squirrels to eat acorns, the oak tree population might actually increase or be unaffected.

answerOptions.text: The hawk population would decrease due to a lack of food.
answerOptions.rationale: Hawks depend on squirrels for food in this ecosystem, so a decline in their primary food source would lead to a decline in the hawk population.

answerOptions.text: The population of bacteria and fungi would increase.
answerOptions.rationale: Decomposers would have less to break down initially if the hawk population also decreases.

answerOptions.text: The amount of sunlight would increase.
answerOptions.rationale: The squirrel population has no effect on the amount of sunlight.

questions.questionText: Based on the completed Punnett square, what is the genotype ratio of the offspring?

answerOptions.rationale: The four outcomes are one GG, two Gg, and one gg.
The completed square includes two heterozygous outcomes, not three GG outcomes.
Only one GG square appears in the passage.
Only one gg square appears in the completed square.

questions.questionText: In the element table, lithium has an atomic mass of 7 and 3 protons. What value should replace X for the number of neutrons in lithium?

answerOptions.rationale: Three is the number of protons, not the number of neutrons.
Neutrons equal atomic mass minus protons, so 7 − 3 = 4.
Seven is the atomic mass, not the neutron count.
This does not match the relationship shown in the table.

questions.questionText: What is the initial evaporation rate (in mL/min) for the high-speed fan (between 0 and 20 minutes)? Round to the nearest tenth.

answerOptions.rationale: The volume decreased from 250 mL at 0 minutes to 198 mL at 20 minutes. The change in volume is 250 - 198 = 52 mL. The change in time is 20 - 0 = 20 minutes. The rate is 52 mL / 20 min = 2.6 mL/min.

questions.questionText: Which station should serve as the control when evaluating the effect of fertilizer runoff on dissolved oxygen?

answerOptions.rationale: It is unaffected by farms or town runoff, so it isolates natural conditions.
This is the impacted site, not the control.
This location adds new variables (treated wastewater).
Controls must be a single baseline condition.

questions.passage: Students investigate whether increased fertilizer runoff is lowering dissolved oxygen (DO) in a river. They set three sample stations: upstream forest, midstream near farms, and downstream near a town outflow. Each day they record water temperature, nitrates (mg/L), and DO (mg/L). They also note that algae blooms appear most frequently near the farms.

questions.questionText: If nitrates spike midstream while temperature stays constant, which claim best explains a drop in dissolved oxygen?

answerOptions.text: Cooler water upstream dissolves less oxygen.
answerOptions.rationale: Cooler water dissolves more oxygen, not less.

answerOptions.text: Excess nutrients fuel algae that consume oxygen when they decompose.
answerOptions.rationale: Decomposition of algal blooms lowers DO.

answerOptions.text: Higher river velocity midstream forces oxygen out of solution.
answerOptions.rationale: Faster water usually increases oxygen mixing.
Town runoff is downstream, not midstream.
